ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "lsoa_code" TEXT /* LSOA code for the incident location */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough where the incident occurred */,
  "major_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the major category of the incident (e.g., Violence Against the Person) */,
  "minor_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the minor category of the incident (e.g., Common Assault) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the incident occurred */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month the incident occurred */,
  "value" INTEGER /* Number of incidents reported */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Exact date of the incident */,
  "incident_time" TEXT /* Exact time of the incident */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was reported */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the incident (e.g., open, in progress, closed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the police officer handling the incident */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific location of the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_minor_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("minor_category_id") REFERENCES minor_categories ("minor_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES incident_locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_major_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("major_category_id") REFERENCES major_categories ("major_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_officer_id FOREIGN KEY ("officer_id") REFERENCES officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough (e.g., Tower Hamlets, Southwark) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the borough in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "major_categories" (
  "major_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each major category */,
  "major_category_name" TEXT /* Name of the major category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the major category */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the major category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("major_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "minor_categories" (
  "minor_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each minor category */,
  "minor_category_name" TEXT /* Name of the minor category */,
  "major_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the major category the minor category belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the minor category */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the minor category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("minor_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_minor_categories_major_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("major_category_id") REFERENCES major_categories ("major_category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "lsoa_code" TEXT /* LSOA code for the location */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough the location belongs to */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_locations_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., police officer, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the incident */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the incident */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each officer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the officer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the officer */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the officer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the officer */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the borough the officer is assigned to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_officers_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., police officer, analyst, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the update */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update (e.g., status change, description update) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_updates_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_updates_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
